title: Clitandra parvifolia
intro: Clitandra parvifolia is a woody climber native to the tropical rainforests of West Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its potential antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Clitandra parvifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Clitandra parvifolia is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to treat various ailments, and its leaves and roots are also used in folk medicine to make teas and ointments.
